questions.q: M. K. Vainu Bappu is celebrated as a pioneer in which field of science?
questions.explanation: Vainu Bappu was an astronomer who is credited with reviving modern observational astronomy in India.

questions.q: Vainu Bappu is best known for reviving modern observational astronomy in which country?
questions.explanation: He is widely regarded as the father of modern Indian astronomy for rebuilding the country's observational facilities.

questions.q: A major optical telescope in India is named in honour of Vainu Bappu. What is it called?
questions.explanation: The Vainu Bappu Telescope at Kavalur is named after him.

questions.q: Vainu Bappu primarily studied which kinds of objects in the night sky?
questions.explanation: As an astronomer he studied stars, comets and other celestial objects.

questions.q: The Vainu Bappu Telescope is located at an observatory in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Vainu Bappu Observatory is at Kavalur in Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: What type of telescope is the Vainu Bappu Telescope named after him?
questions.explanation: The Vainu Bappu Telescope is a large optical telescope used for visible-light astronomy.

questions.q: Vainu Bappu co-discovered a relationship that helps astronomers measure distances. The relationship deals with which kind of stars?
questions.explanation: The Wilson-Bappu effect relates a stellar spectral feature to a star's luminosity, helping estimate distances.

questions.q: Early in his career, Vainu Bappu co-discovered an object travelling through the solar system in 1949. What kind of object was it?
questions.explanation: He was a co-discoverer of Comet Bappu-Bok-Newkirk in 1949.

questions.q: Vainu Bappu was Indian by nationality. In which country was he born?
questions.explanation: He was born in India, in Madras (present-day Chennai).

questions.q: Vainu Bappu played a key leadership role in building up which kind of national scientific facilities in India?
questions.explanation: He led the establishment and growth of modern Indian observatories such as Kodaikanal and Kavalur.

questions.q: What was M. K. Vainu Bappu's full name?
questions.explanation: His full name was Manali Kallat Vainu Bappu.

questions.q: In which year was Vainu Bappu born?
questions.explanation: He was born on 10 August 1927 in Madras.

questions.q: Vainu Bappu earned his doctorate in astronomy from which prestigious institution?
questions.explanation: He completed his PhD at Harvard University in the United States.

questions.q: The stellar relationship Vainu Bappu co-discovered with American astronomer Olin Wilson is commonly called what?
questions.explanation: It is known as the Wilson-Bappu effect, linking a chromospheric emission line width to stellar luminosity.

questions.q: The Wilson-Bappu effect is based on a measurable feature in a star's spectrum involving which element?
questions.explanation: The effect uses the width of the Calcium II K emission line, which correlates with the star's absolute magnitude.

questions.q: Vainu Bappu was a founder and the first president of which scientific society in his home country?
questions.explanation: He was a founder and the first president of the Astronomical Society of India.

questions.q: Vainu Bappu was a long-serving director of which historic Indian observatory before establishing new facilities?
questions.explanation: He became director of the Kodaikanal Observatory and modernised India's solar and stellar astronomy from there.

questions.q: The premier astronomy research institute that grew out of Vainu Bappu's work is known today as which organisation?
questions.explanation: He was the founding director of the Indian Institute of Astrophysics in Bangalore.

questions.q: Vainu Bappu chose Kavalur as an observing site primarily because of what advantage?
options: Its dark skies suitable for optical astronomy
questions.explanation: Kavalur offered dark, relatively clear skies well-suited to optical observations.

questions.q: In which year did Vainu Bappu pass away?
questions.explanation: He died on 19 August 1982 at the age of 55.

questions.q: Vainu Bappu held a high-profile international leadership role in the global astronomy community. Which post did he hold?
options: President of the International Astronomical Union
questions.explanation: He served as President of the International Astronomical Union (IAU).

questions.q: Apart from being a research scientist, Vainu Bappu was especially known for which contribution to Indian astronomy?
questions.explanation: His lasting legacy is the establishment of modern, well-equipped observatories that put India on the astronomical map.

questions.q: The Vainu Bappu Telescope at Kavalur has a primary mirror of approximately what diameter?
questions.explanation: The Vainu Bappu Telescope has a 2.34-metre (about 93-inch) primary mirror, long among the largest in Asia.

questions.q: When commissioned in the mid-1980s, the Vainu Bappu Telescope was notable for being one of the largest optical telescopes in which region?
questions.explanation: It was for many years the largest optical telescope in Asia.

questions.q: The 1949 comet that Vainu Bappu co-discovered also bears the names of two other astronomers. They were:
questions.explanation: The comet is named Bappu-Bok-Newkirk after its three co-discoverers.

questions.q: Vainu Bappu's father, Manali Kukuzhi Bappu, also worked in astronomy as a:
questions.explanation: His father was an astronomer at the Nizamiah Observatory in Hyderabad, giving Vainu an early exposure to the field.

questions.q: Early in his career in the United States, Vainu Bappu received the Donohoe Comet Medal of the Astronomical Society of the Pacific for which achievement?
options: Calculating the age of the universe
questions.explanation: He was awarded the Donohoe Comet Medal for his role in the discovery of the comet that bears his name.

questions.q: The Wilson-Bappu effect is particularly valuable to astronomers because the relationship is largely independent of which stellar property?
options: The star's spectral type or temperature
questions.explanation: The correlation between the Ca II K line width and luminosity holds across a wide range of stellar types, making it a useful distance indicator.

questions.q: Vainu Bappu was honoured by India with which civilian award for his contributions to science?
questions.explanation: He was awarded the Padma Bhushan, one of India's high civilian honours, for his services to science.

questions.q: Which of the following best describes Vainu Bappu's overall significance to Indian science?
options: He founded India's nuclear weapons programme
He revived observational astronomy and built world-class observatories in India
He discovered a vaccine for a major disease
He designed India's first satellite launch vehicle
questions.explanation: His enduring legacy lies in reviving modern observational astronomy and creating institutions and telescopes that established India in global astronomy.
